526.name: M60_FI_NEAR60+Top3 (cc3_527.py)
526.description: A trend-following rotator that uses QQQ as a regime signal and allocates across leveraged equity, large-cap stocks, and cash based on a composite score of four conditions. Each day it counts how many of four signals are true — a median-price trend gate, 60-day momentum, a positive force index, and proximity to the 60-day high — and maps that count to a graduated allocation ladder. The universe side-pocket holds the top three large-cap stocks by market cap, which absorb allocation in the middle-risk tiers.
526.overfit_notes: Median uses the 101st value of the last 200 closes (not a round SMA); force index sums 13 bars; near-60 threshold is 95% of the 60-day high; the 5-level allocation ladder (100/0/0, 70/30/0, 30/70/0, 0/50/50, 0/0/100) is hand-tuned. Four combined signals and the TQQQ execution vehicle add overfit pressure.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median close of last 200 days (f0)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f1: QQQ close[-1] > close[-61] (60-day momentum)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f2: 13-bar price×volume force index > 0

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f3: QQQ price ≥ 95% of 60-day high

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-3 large-cap stocks by market cap / BIL

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume → top 3 by market cap (fine selection), refreshed daily

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; only trades when score n changes

527.name: M60_FI_VOLCONT+Top3 (cc3_528.py)
527.description: A trend-following rotator identical in structure to M60_FI_NEAR60 but replaces the near-60-high proximity filter with a volatility contraction condition. The third signal checks whether short-term realized volatility has compressed below its longer-term baseline, indicating a calmer market environment. Allocation flows across TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and BIL on a five-level ladder driven by how many conditions are simultaneously true.
527.overfit_notes: Volatility contraction compares 10-bar stddev against 60-bar stddev — both window lengths are non-canonical; force index uses 13 bars; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes; the 5-level allocation step function is hand-tuned. TQQQ as execution vehicle adds overfit pressure.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median close of last 200 days (f0)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f1: QQQ close[-1] > close[-61] (60-day momentum)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f2: 13-bar price×volume force index > 0

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f3: 10-bar realized vol < 60-bar realized vol (volatility contraction)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-3 large-cap stocks by market cap / BIL

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume → top 3 by market cap (fine selection), refreshed daily

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; only trades when score n changes

528.name: M60_FI_UPVOL+Top3 (cc3_529.py)
528.description: A trend-following rotator that replaces the near-high proximity filter with an up-volume dominance condition. The third signal checks whether cumulative volume on up-days has exceeded cumulative volume on down-days over the recent period, indicating broad buying pressure. The same five-level ladder maps the composite score to allocations across TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and cash.
528.overfit_notes: Up-volume window is 20 bars; force index window is 13 bars; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes; 60-day momentum lookback; the 5-level allocation step function is hand-tuned. TQQQ execution adds overfit pressure.

rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-3 large-cap stocks by market cap / BIL

529.name: M60_NEAR60_VOLCONT+Top3 (cc3_530.py)
529.description: A trend-following rotator that swaps the force index signal for a near-60-high proximity filter, retaining 60-day momentum and volatility contraction as the other two conditions. The strategy checks whether QQQ is trading near its recent high (strength) and whether short-term volatility has compressed (calm environment) before adding risk. The five-level allocation ladder blends TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and BIL based on the composite score.
529.overfit_notes: Near-60 threshold is 95% of the 60-day max; volatility contraction compares 10-bar and 60-bar stddev windows; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes; the 5-level allocation step function and TQQQ execution are non-generic choices.

Allocations step across TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and BIL based on how many of the four conditions are met.
530.overfit_notes: Near-60 threshold is 95% of the 60-day max; up-volume window is 20 bars; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes; 60-day momentum lookback; the 5-level allocation step function and TQQQ execution are non-generic choices.

531.name: M60_VOLCONT_UPVOL+Top3 (cc3_532.py)
531.description: A trend-following rotator pairing volatility contraction and up-volume dominance as its two confirmation signals alongside 60-day momentum and the median-price regime gate. The strategy requires a simultaneously calm (compressing volatility) and buyer-driven (up-volume excess) market before adding leveraged exposure. Allocations graduate across TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and BIL based on the composite score.
531.overfit_notes: Volatility contraction compares 10-bar and 60-bar stddev windows; up-volume window is 20 bars; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes; 60-day momentum lookback; the 5-level allocation step function and TQQQ execution are non-generic.

532.name: M20_RNG_NEAR60+Top3 (cc3_533.py)
532.description: A trend-following rotator that uses a shorter 20-day momentum lookback and adds a daily-range expansion filter alongside a near-60-day-high proximity check. The range expansion signal fires when recent average true range exceeds the longer-term baseline, indicating elevated but not panicked activity. The same five-level ladder maps the composite score to allocations across TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and BIL.
532.overfit_notes: Range expansion compares 25-bar average H-L to the 200-bar average multiplied by 1.1 — both the window lengths and the 1.1 multiplier are tuned; near-60 threshold is 95%; 20-day momentum lookback is shorter than typical; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median close of last 200 days (f0)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f1: QQQ close[-1] > close[-21] (20-day momentum)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f2: 25-bar avg daily range > 200-bar avg daily range × 1.1 (range expansion)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f3: QQQ price ≥ 95% of 60-day high

rules.label: Symbols

533.description: A trend-following rotator using 20-day momentum, range expansion, and volatility contraction as its three confirmation signals. The combination of expanding ranges alongside compressing short-term volatility attempts to identify calm breakout environments. Allocations move across TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and BIL on a five-level ladder.
533.overfit_notes: Range expansion compares 25-bar and 200-bar average H-L with a 1.1 multiplier; volatility contraction compares 10-bar and 60-bar stddev windows; 20-day momentum lookback; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes. Multiple tuned parameters push overfit toward 4/10.

534.name: M20_WVF_NEAR60+Top3 (cc3_535.py)
534.description: A trend-following rotator combining 20-day momentum with two strength filters: a Williams VIX Fix proxy (proximity to the 22-day high) and a near-60-day-high check. When QQQ is trading close to both its short-term and medium-term highs simultaneously, the strategy interprets this as a strong, sustained trend and maximizes leveraged exposure. Allocation steps down through TQQQ, large-cap stocks, and BIL as conditions weaken.
534.overfit_notes: WVF threshold is within 5% of the 22-day high (non-canonical window); near-60 threshold is 95% of the 60-day max; 20-day momentum lookback; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes. The dual near-high combination is a specific structural choice.

537.name: M252_FI_NEAR60+Top3 (cc3_538.py)
537.description: A trend-following rotator using a full-year momentum lookback alongside a force index and a near-60-day-high filter. By requiring that the current price exceed the price from one year ago, the strategy targets only strongly trending annual regimes before layering on shorter-term confirmation. The five-level allocation ladder steps through TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and BIL based on how many conditions hold.
537.overfit_notes: Momentum compares close[-1] vs close[0] over the full 252-bar history window (1-year lookback); force index sums 13 bars; near-60 threshold is 95% of the 60-day max; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes. The ladder structure and TQQQ execution are non-generic.

539.name: M252_UPVOL_VOLCONT+Top3 (cc3_540.py)
539.description: A trend-following rotator that anchors on a full-year momentum gate and adds two market-structure conditions: up-volume dominance over down-volume and volatility contraction. The strategy looks for annual uptrends sustained by broad buyer participation while volatility is compressing — a setup associated with healthy, durable rallies. The five-level ladder steps exposure from TQQQ through large-cap stocks to cash.
